title: Dashboard and Saved Search Usage Analytics
dataSources: Dashboard view and search run audit logs
spl:
index=_audit action=view OR action=run
| search (resource_type="dashboard" OR resource_type="saved_search")
| stats count by user, resource_name, resource_type
| sort -count
description: Usage of dashboards and saved searches guides optimization and retirement. Analytics support adoption and reduce stale or unused content.
value: Usage of dashboards and saved searches guides optimization and retirement. Analytics support adoption and reduce stale or unused content.
implementation: Ingest Splunk audit or usage logs for dashboard and search runs. Report on most/least used dashboards and searches. Identify unused content for archival. Track adoption by team.
visualization: Bar chart (views by dashboard), Table (search run count by name), Pie chart (usage by user).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk audit logs, usage metadata.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Dashboard view and search run audit logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest Splunk audit or usage logs for dashboard and search runs. Report on most/least used dashboards and searches. Identify unused content for archival. Track adoption by team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit action=view OR action=run
| search (resource_type="dashboard" OR resource_type="saved_search")
| stats count by user, resource_name, resource_type
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Dashboard and Saved Search Usage Analytics** — Usage of dashboards and saved searches guides optimization and retirement. Analytics support adoption and reduce stale or unused content.

Documented **Data sources**: Dashboard view and search run audit logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk audit logs, usage metadata. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, resource_name, resource_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range you can explain. Compare with the closest Grafana dashboard, APM UI, or Splunk monitoring console, and spot-check a few raw rows for fields that matter.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (views by dashboard), Table (search run count by name), Pie chart (usage by user).
grandmaExplanation: We see which views and stored searches people actually use so we can drop clutter and keep the right work maintained.
knownFalsePositives: Admin spikes from automation, break-glass, and app pushes. We check change records before a single run looks like a takeover.
